title: Fired Up!
short: A chaotic 2v2 couch co-op game jam entry where you race against teammates and opponents to build the biggest prehistoric campfire using clever sabotage and resource wrangling.
description: Fired Up! is a fast-paced, multiplayer couch co-op experience developed as a group project during the DAE Game Projects 2025. Teaming up (or competing) in pairs, players must collect resources—wood from trees, tar from pits, or even steal from rivals—to fuel their campfire before time runs out or it extinguishes. Far from cozy, this prehistoric frenzy lets players ram opponents to make them drop items, launch tar to slow them down, and topple trees as obstacles. Fully playable on a single screen with four Xbox controllers, the game blends survival, strategy, and mayhem into a short but memorable jam creation
contribution:
• Implemented co-op systems 
• Added numerous amounts of VFX & SFX 
• Made scene transition system. 
• Made pushable obstacle system. 
• Made World-Space UI elements.

title: Sci-ANT-ology
short: A game-jam action adventure where you play an ant missionary spreading the fungal faith of Mycenu while resisting corruption.
description: Sci-ANT-ology is a game-jam project created in under 48 hours, where you step into the role of Fun Gus, a missionary ant chosen by the lord of mold, Mycenu. Your task is to evangelize the ant citizens of Ants-werp, spreading the fungal faith while fending off forces that seek to corrupt or silence your followers. Built collaboratively during the Unwrap Jam 2025, the game blends quirky humor with fast-paced action, showcasing a unique theme of cultish devotion, fungal mysticism, and grassroots evangelism—all packed into a short but memorable jam experience.
contribution:
• Flocking system of NPCs
• Implemented AI behaviours
• Basic movement mechanics 
• Designed code structure for

title: Kirby's Adventure remake
short: A C++ game project recreating Kirby with custom controls, powers, levels, and scene management, built as part of the DAE Programming 2 course.
description:
This Programming 2 assignment is my personal take on recreating a classic Kirby game in C++. The project implements core gameplay systems including character movement, jumping, flying, and a flexible power system that allows easy addition of new abilities. I also built a scene and level manager for smooth transitions, with support for game states such as victory and game over screens.

 From a technical perspective, the project demonstrates solid use of object composition and inheritance. For example, an AnimationController manages multiple Animation objects, while entities like Kirby, enemies, and the player all inherit from a shared Entity class. Powers are encapsulated into a Power class, enabling both Kirby and enemies to share abilities. Enemies such as Waddle Dee, Sparky, and Hot Head all derive from a base Enemy class, making behavior extensible.

 Additional planned features included particle effects, a hub world, and advanced movement mechanics like running. The project was developed in Visual Studio 2022, showcasing both programming fundamentals and scalable architecture in a game context.

title: Digger Remake
short: A modern remake of the arcade classic Digger, built in C++ with a custom SDL2 engine featuring co-op play, pathfinding enemies, and a component-based architecture.
description:
This project is a modern remake of the classic arcade game Digger, developed in C++ using a custom game engine (Minigin, adapted as Rinigin for the assignment) powered by SDL2. The game challenges players to dig tunnels, collect emeralds and gold, and survive against enemy Nobbins, which use pathfinding AI to hunt the player. It supports both solo and co-op play, with players sharing lives across three levels. Scoring is based on collecting items and defeating enemies, with a persistent high-score system that saves player initials.

On the technical side, the engine implements several foundational game development patterns and systems:
- Component-based architecture for modular entity design.
- State pattern for flexible state management via a StateContextComponent.
- Observer pattern with an event system for decoupled communication.
- Command pattern powering a fully command-based input system.
- XInput implementation for gamepad support (Xbox controllers) integrated with the command-based input layer.
- Prototype pattern for efficient entity management.
- Service Locator–based audio system using SDL_Mixer.
- 2D physics system supporting rigidbodies.

This project highlights both engine programming and gameplay implementation, demonstrating knowledge of design patterns, architecture, and core game loop systems while delivering a faithful but modernized take on an arcade classic.
